Even if there are no problems with each individual segment, there is still a sense of discomfort when looking at the text as a whole.

Sub_Theme: Limitations of feedback within a limited time
Text:
- SU_23_149:I think this feedback was given because the person giving it remembered ‘I taught this student how to take arterial blood samples’. As we can see similar feedback in Index number 153, I suspect that the person giving the feedback took the time to compile it.
- SU_23_151:I felt that for a surgeon who is extremely busy, it may be difficult to have the determination to take the time to give feedback on a simple record.
- SU_23_153:The feedback was almost the same as Index number 149.

Sub_Theme: Conflict of interest as an educator
Text: - SU_10_108:I felt that it would be beneficial to encourage learners to learn how to match up their own examination findings with the findings of the examination. The contrast between the learners' findings and the instructor's findings is also thought to have been made in the same context, but it seems to show the superiority and infallibility of the instructor, and I think that this is feedback that makes learners feel an authority gradient.

Sub_Theme: Insufficient amount of description
Text:
- SU_04_001:I felt the feedback was too short. The good points are that it mentions the content of the description, so you can tell that it has been read and written. The points that need to be improved are that it does not mention all the content of the description, and the feedback feels insufficient.
- SU_04_002:The feedback seems very short. The good points are that it reads the content of the medical student's description. The points that need to be improved are that the only content is ‘I will teach you in the future’, and I feel that it is not content that will improve the learning motivation of the medical student who wrote the description.
- SU_04_010:The feedback is short in relation to the medical students‘ entries. The good points are that the medical students’ independence is being developed. The points for improvement are that I think the motivation would increase if feedback was given on what they had learnt and what their future challenges are.
- SU_05_018:(Good points) The feedback is based on an awareness of the actual patient's condition and the experience that goes with it. (Points for improvement) The amount of description is small. The content of the suggestions is a little imprecise.
- SU_05_026:Although it is a short comment, it does mention the good points.
- SU_09_085:Although it was not very long and not very specific, I think it was encouraging feedback.

Sub_Theme: Insufficient amount of description
Text:

- SU_15_020:The impression is that the feedback is brief and minimal. The gratitude and encouragement for the record is fully felt, but it would be desirable to have more specific feedback.

Sub_Theme: Insufficient amount of description
Text:
- SU_05_020:The supportive aspects are good, but it's just too short. However, if the supervising doctor and student are communicating well on the spot, then this is not a problem.
- SU_05_021:The student has written a long piece of text, but the feedback is brief, and I think the student may be worried that the supervising doctor hasn't read it even though they wrote it so carefully and at such length. On the other hand, it is a good point that it mentions learning in conjunction with matters that are of great interest to students, such as the national exam.
- SU_05_031:Good points: The fact that the medical record is evaluated and that communication with residents and others is evaluated is clearly conveyed. Points for improvement: It is too short.
- SU_05_033:The student's record is also simple, so it is not possible to know the results, but it is too short.
- SU_09_094:I don't think a simple ‘Thank you for your hard work’ would be considered feedback.
- SU_11_122:It is too businesslike and directive. I wonder if there was anything else you wanted to convey? I wonder if there was anything you wanted to praise?
- SU_11_131:It's too simple and doesn't amount to feedback.
- SU_14_001:The fact that it is detailed and thorough every day is encouraging. On the other hand, the sentences are short and there is no mention of my own areas for improvement, so I'm not really sure what I need to improve to do better.
- SU_14_002:I think it's good that it accurately addresses future issues. One point for improvement is that the amount of text is small, so it's not really feedback.
- SU_16_042:The impression is that it is just a comment. There is very little written. I think it is good that it shows empathy with the difficulty of learning. On the other hand, it is a shame that the content is very little and abstract.
- SU_16_044:The impression is that it is not possible to give feedback with just a few words. It is concise and easy to understand, but I think it would be better if it were more detailed.
- SU_16_048:It seems that only one evaluation point is being written. While it is good that the evaluation is easy to understand, there is too little information to lead to improvement.
- SU_21_128:There was no feedback on what was specifically evaluated. It was short.
- SU_21_129:There was no feedback on what was specifically evaluated. It was short.

Text: - SU_23_164:The clinical practice records were kept well, and since the exact same content as this feedback was sent to another student, it is thought that this was copied and pasted feedback.

The good points are that the content not only refers to what was written, but also mentions the students‘ attitude during the practical training and the content of their presentations. The improvement point is that there was no comment on the nephrotic syndrome, which was written about as something that had been learned.
- SU_05_019:(Good points) It is clear that the comments are based on the actual experience of the student, not just on the content of the student's description. It is supportive content for the student. (Bad points) The comments and advice are about the student's training itself, and there is little mention of the content of the record. However, I think this is unavoidable, as it is thought that this feedback from the supervising doctor is more about the training itself than about the content of the record. In fact, it is more important to give feedback on the training itself than on the content of the record.
- SU_05_030:This is partly because the student's record content is limited to listing the names of surgeries and lacks specificity, but it does not seem that the comments are based on the content of the record. It is short.
- SU_06_049:This is not feedback on the record, but a message about the practical training.
- SU_07_064:The points that are being raised are detailed and specific, but I think that feedback that covers the whole is necessary.
- SU_07_068:I can understand what the assessor is trying to say, but this is not a comprehensive record feedback.
- SU_09_095:The feedback is more supportive, as it is based on the student's behaviour during the practical training, rather than on the contents of the portfolio.
- SU_09_097:The feedback is specific and based on the student's behaviour during the practical training. There are some parts that do not necessarily match the contents of the portfolio.
- SU_09_099:Although the portfolio is hardly mentioned, the FB is based on the student's attitude during the practical training and the content observed.
- SU_12_143:As an evaluation by many supervisors, I can relate to the content of this. As the student has written quite specific details, I thought it would be good to touch on that point a little more.
- SU_13_150:Good points: The feedback draws the trainee's attention to important long-term issues that they may not have been aware of, and also touches on the inadequacy of the practical training record. Bad points: The feedback on daily work is vague, with no specific description. There is no feedback on the trainee's learning HIE images or on respiratory management, which was listed as a future issue.
- SU_13_160:It seems that the learner and the instructor have not shared the purpose of this learning record in advance, or that there is a custom of just writing down what you did and getting a stamp, or that the learner does not expect any feedback from the instructor. Good points: Feedback is given on how to make a better record. Improvement points: There is no mention of the learner's attitude to learning or the malignant thymoma mentioned in the record.
- SU_13_161:No mention of the learning record, but there is mention of the learning attitude and training in the surgical field. Good points: As a mentor, the trainer conveys the appeal of surgical training. Improvement points: There is no feedback on the learning record.
- SU_13_164:It does not mention the learning attitude or the content of the record at all. It is a mechanical response. Good points: None. Improvement points: Include proper feedback that mentions the learning attitude and the content of the record.
- SU_15_031:The impression is that it is concise feedback on the practical training. While it is good that it comments on the attitude towards the practical training, there is very little mention of the practical training record itself, so it would be good if there was some specific feedback.
- SU_17_054:I doubt whether the person reading the feedback is even a medical student, so it should be improved.
- SU_19_092:The main focus is on feedback on the students’ behaviour during the training, and I don't think there is any feedback based on the entries.
- SU_19_095:It seems to me that the feedback is mainly about the student's behaviour during the practical training, and there is not much feedback on the records. As the content that the student achieved during the practical training is written in detail, I think it would be better if there were further issues presented.
- SU_19_098:There is an evaluation of the level of achievement and attitude during practice, but there is little feedback that refers to the records.
- SU_20_101:I feel that the content of the actual conference is mentioned, and that the students are being evaluated through the practical training. I think it is good that it is positive feedback. As the content of the record is not mentioned, I think it would be even better if the content was also mentioned.
- SU_21_124:The feedback was based on the actual content of the practical training, rather than on the students' records. It would be better if there was more feedback on the learning records.

Sub_Theme: Feedback that does not reflect the content of the report
Text:
- SU_13_152:The report focuses on the learner's experience and provides appropriate feedback. It raises issues related to the communication of more practical findings and the outcome of the disease other than the examination of the case. It does not touch on the points for improvement in the learner's report itself. The points for improvement are formative feedback on a better report
- SU_13_154:Indicates the rarity of the case experienced, while also recommending learning of typical cases. The improvement point is that there is no indication of deficiencies in the report.
- SU_13_158:The impression is that the feedback is based on sharing the experience of the practical training. It is good that there is feedback supporting the attitude of the practical training, the techniques used, and the practice for the future. There is no mention or feedback on the trainee's record, so this needs to be improved.

Sub_Theme: Feedback that does not reflect the content of the report
Code: Lack of indication of points for description
Text:
- SU_14_008:Feedback was given on the content of the practical training, and it was easy to understand what to be careful of in future practical training. There was no feedback on the practical training record.
- SU_15_021:The impression is that the feedback is very general. Although the student is thanked for the practical training and comments are made, there is little specific reference to the content of the practical training or the record, and it cannot be said that this is feedback that can be used for future practical training or records.

Sub_Theme: Feedback that does not reflect the content of the report
Code: Lack of reference to the content of the description
Text:
- SU_19_094:There is no mention of the student records or clinical practice standards, and there is no reflection on the practical training or presentation of future learning issues. From the perspective of contributing to student learning, I get the impression that it is not quite appropriate.
- SU_19_097:There is little feedback that refers to the student's records or the standards for clinical practice. However, the knowledge of diseases that is useful for diagnosis is shared, and I think this is useful feedback for students. There is also a phrase that asks about hopes for future practice, and I felt that this could encourage students to take an active part.

Sub_Theme: Feedback that does not reflect the content of the report
Code: Disregard for the content of the description
Text:
- SU_10_109:There is no mention of the content of the practical training or the learner's own description. It is impossible to know what is not written, so there is a possibility that important feedback is being given without being written down, but I think it would be good to make sure that important feedback points are recorded.
- SU_10_115:The student's description itself is concerned with the everyday details of the case (fever follow-up) and less important technical points (the speed of spinal fluid output), so I think this is a difficult case for feedback. In cases like this, instead of giving bland feedback that ignores the content of the description, it would be better if the feedback could point out the appropriate learning points for the specialist field.
- SU_11_118:Compared to feedback1, there is no feedback in the practical training record. On the other hand, there is follow-up on the student's attitude during the practical training and the fact that they were unable to give a good presentation, and the fact that they made an effort to improve the content of their presentation is praised, so it feels like feedback that has looked closely at the practical training as a whole.
- SU_11_119:It gives the impression that the busy supervising doctor quickly wrote down their impressions of the student. They probably evaluated the student as having met the required standard with no particular problems when compared to the behavioural standards, but they did not evaluate the record that was written. It seems to be just a summary evaluation conclusion, so it is not thought to contribute to improving skills and attitude.
- SU_11_128:The quantity and quality of the records seems to be lacking, but there is no comment on this. There are no comments on the content of the records.
